name: Caret Legal (AbacusLaw) Practice Management API
what_it_does: Caret Legal (formerly AbacusLaw, rebranded 2022) law practice management REST API for small and mid-size law firms with case management, client intake, billing, and court calendar management. Enables AI agents to manage case and matter creation for law practice case management automation, handle client and contact management for legal CRM automation, access task and deadline tracking for attorney docket management automation, retrieve billing and time entry for law firm billing automation, manage document management and email filing for legal document workflow automation, handle court rule calendaring and deadline calculation for litigation deadline automation, access conflict of interest checking for client intake workflow automation, retrieve trust accounting and IOLTA ledger management for law firm financial compliance automation, manage firm-wide calendar and court date management for legal scheduling automation, and integrate Caret Legal with Outlook, NetDocuments, and legal research platforms for full law practice management.
use_cases: Managing cases from legal workflow agents
Tracking deadlines from docket management agents
Processing billing from time and billing agents
Checking conflicts from client intake agents
not_for: Large law firm enterprise (use iManage, Elite, or Aderant)
Contract lifecycle management (use Ironclad or ContractPodAi)
best_when: A small to mid-size law firm (1-50 attorneys) wanting AI agents to automate case management, court deadline calendaring, conflict checking, billing, and IOLTA trust accounting within Caret Legal/AbacusLaw.
avoid_when: IOLTA TRUST ACCOUNTING THREE-WAY RECONCILIATION: Automated trust accounting in Caret Legal must implement strict three-way reconciliation (trust ledger, bank statement, client ledger) per state bar IOLTA rules; automated trust balance adjustments without three-way reconciliation creates bar ethics violation with potential disbarment. COURT RULE DEADLINES JURISDICTIONAL ACCURACY: Automated court rule calendaring for litigation deadlines must use jurisdiction-specific court rules (Federal FRCP, state court local rules, appellate rules); automated deadline calculation using wrong jurisdiction rules creates missed deadline, default judgment, and legal malpractice exposure. CONFLICT OF INTEREST CHECK LEGAL SUFFICIENCY: Automated conflict checking must search all parties (adverse parties, related entities, witnesses) in active and archived matters; automated conflict check limited to current client names without historical matter archive creates ethics conflict exposure and disqualification risk.

auth.notes: Caret Legal uses API key and OAuth authentication. REST API with JSON. Caret Legal is the rebranded name for AbacusLaw (2022 rebranding by Caret Group). Originally AbacusLaw founded 1983 as Abacus Data Systems. Caret Group also owns Amicus Attorney, HoudiniEsq, and other legal tech products. Small to mid-size law firm PM focus. Court rules calendar engine. IOLTA trust accounting. 50,000+ law firm users historically. Competes with Clio, MyCase, and PracticePanther for small firm practice management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-user annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-user subscription; core practice management, billing, and trust accounting; court rules calendaring add-on
pricing.notes: Caret Group subsidiary (rebranded from AbacusLaw 2022). Founded 1983. Small to mid-size law firms. Per-user annual subscription. IOLTA trust accounting included.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; matter and billing queries throttled; no webhooks — polling required

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — MATTER AND BILLING EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: Caret Legal does not support native webhooks; automated case management workflows, billing triggers, and trust accounting events require scheduled polling; design polling intervals aligned with law firm daily billing review and calendar management cycles

IOLTA TRUST THREE-WAY RECONCILIATION REQUIRED: Automated trust accounting in Caret Legal must maintain three-way reconciliation between trust bank account, client trust ledger, and firm trust account ledger; automated trust disbursement or balance adjustments without three-way reconciliation creates state bar IOLTA rule violation; implement reconciliation verification before any automated trust transaction

COURT RULES CALENDAR JURISDICTION ACCURACY: Automated deadline calculation using Caret Legal court rules calendar must verify correct jurisdiction selection (federal district, state court, appellate) for each matter; automated deadline calculation using incorrect jurisdiction or outdated court rules creates missed deadline and legal malpractice exposure

Caret Group product portfolio rebranding confusion — Caret Legal (AbacusLaw), Amicus Attorney, and HoudiniEsq are all Caret Group products with distinct APIs; verify which Caret product a specific firm uses before API implementation; AbacusLaw/Caret Legal and Amicus Attorney APIs are not interchangeable despite same parent company

Conflict check historical matter archive completeness — automated conflict of interest search must include closed and archived matters, not only active cases; Caret Legal conflict check scope configuration must include full historical database; automated client intake without historical conflict check creates conflicts from legacy matters

Document management local server vs cloud storage — AbacusLaw/Caret Legal legacy installations may use local network file storage for document management; automated document management integration must determine whether firm uses local storage or cloud document management (NetDocuments, SharePoint); automated document access against local storage requires network/VPN access from automation host
agent_readiness.security_notes: Law practice management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US/CA. Client matter, trust accounting, and privileged legal data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Law practice management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US/CA. Client matter, IOLTA trust, and privileged legal data.
